highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 3.119.0 is the first stable 3.x release, built on Skia m119 with new architectures, D3D on Windows and SkCanvas.SaveLayerRec.
highlights_body: It rolls up the 3.118.0 previews (Skia m117/m118, HarfBuzz 8.3.1, libexpat 2.6.4, libjpeg-turbo, FreeType) and then bumps to Skia m119, adds Direct3D on Windows, `SkCanvas.SaveLayerRec` and the missing `SKColorFilter` types, extends Linux support with riscv64 and LoongArch64, and lands a stack of Metal, tvOS and Windows ARM fixes.

bullets.lead: Skia bumped through m117, m118 and m119
bullets.detail: Three upstream engine bumps land the m117 baseline, m118 preview and the m119 stable milestone that names the release.
Bumps HarfBuzz to 8.3.1, libexpat to 2.6.4, libpng to 1.6.44, libjpeg-turbo to the 2.1.5.1+commits snapshot and FreeType to 2.13.3+ to pick up upstream fixes.
Refreshes the bundled Vulkan Memory Allocator so the Vulkan backend picks up the latest fixes.

bullets.lead: Unused externals pruned and CVE-2024-30105 addressed
bullets.detail: Disables and removes a set of unused or problematic externals so the shipping surface stays lean, and rolls dependencies to address CVE-2024-30105.
Compiles Skia with the Direct3D backend on Windows so applications can render through D3D in addition to OpenGL and ANGLE.
Exposes `SkCanvas::SaveLayerRec` so callers can create save layers with the full set of `SkRect`, backdrop filter, image filter and layer-flag options.
Adds the color-filter types that were still missing from the managed surface so the color-filter API matches upstream Skia.

bullets.lead: Metal APIs on GRBackendRenderTarget across all platforms
bullets.detail: Exposes the Metal-backed `GRBackendRenderTarget` constructors on every Apple TFM so Metal render targets can be created uniformly from iOS, tvOS, macOS and Mac Catalyst.
Extends the Metal `SKMetalView` control to tvOS so tvOS apps get the same accelerated view as iOS and Mac Catalyst.
Fixes `SKImage.FromPicture` so pictures rasterize into images correctly instead of silently returning wrong content.
Fixes a regression that broke projects consuming SkiaSharp via `packages.config` on .NET Framework and Mono.
Restores Metal performance on the iOS Simulator that had regressed with the earlier Metal backend changes.

Fixes the WASM import path so SkiaSharp loads correctly from Blazor apps hosted under a base path, moves the workaround into the SkiaSharp WASM assets and pins the Windows SDK version deterministically.

bullets.lead: tvOS targets corrected and Apple-only code fenced
bullets.detail: Sets the correct target for tvOS, ships the tvOS external fixes and excludes code that will never run on Apple platforms from the Apple builds.
Uses the correct `PackagePath` attribute so the packaged assets land in the expected NuGet layout.
Adds native build support for `linux-riscv64` and `linux-loongarch64`, and extends `clang-cross` to support Alpine so the Linux binaries cover more architectures.
Enables OpenGL on Windows ARM and fixes the build targets so SkiaSharp runs on ARM64 Windows machines.

bullets.lead: Android NDK bumped to r27c with 16 KB page alignment
bullets.detail: Rebuilds the Android natives on NDK r27c so SkiaSharp keeps loading on Android 15+ devices that use 16 KB memory pages.
Extends the `clang-cross` support to Alpine so the Alpine (musl) Linux builds share the same toolchain path.
Fixes an incorrect internal call in the bindings that could invoke the wrong native entry point in certain code paths.

contributor_summaries.kasperk81: Added `riscv64` build support, fixed the Linux tests, updated the Vulkan Memory Allocator to 3.2.1 and extended `clang-cross` to Alpine
contributor_summaries.Kation: Compiled Skia with the Direct3D backend on Windows
contributor_summaries.kkwpsv: Fixed an incorrect internal call in the bindings
contributor_summaries.pjcollins: Addressed CVE-2024-30105 in the build inputs
contributor_summaries.spouliot: Added the Metal `GRBackendRenderTarget` overloads across every Apple platform
contributor_summaries.sungaila: Fixed the regression that broke projects using .NET Framework/Mono and `packages.config`
contributor_summaries.taublast: Restored iOS Simulator performance with the Metal backend

preview_summaries.p1: Preview 1 rolled up the entire 3.118 preview line and added the m119 engine bump, Direct3D on Windows, `SkCanvas.SaveLayerRec`, the missing `SKColorFilter` types, `SKMetalView` on tvOS, riscv64/LoongArch64/Alpine Linux support, Windows ARM support and the CI/CVE hardening that turned the line into a stable release.
preview_summaries.p2: Preview 2 (from the 3.118 line) stacked the m118 engine bump, rolled HarfBuzz, libexpat, libjpeg-turbo and FreeType, added the Metal `GRBackendRenderTarget` overloads across Apple platforms and fixed the .NET 9 Blazor hosting story.
